seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents in Smolagents Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix context window management for ai agents in Smolagents agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Smolagents, Context Window Management issues arise due to its lightweight design where agents directly generate and execute Python code without built-in token limits or context truncation. Agents often exceed model context windows by accumulating conversation history, tool outputs, and generated code in the `agent.run()` message stream. This manifests as truncated reasoning, forgotten instructions, or complete agent failure when the LLM rejects oversized prompts.

howItManifests.symptom: Agent repeats failed code blocks from earlier turns
howItManifests.impact: Infinite loops generating same broken code, burning tokens

howItManifests.symptom: Agent ignores recent tool results buried in history
howItManifests.impact: Incorrect tool selection and cascading failures

howItManifests.scenario: Code generation with embedded conversation history
howItManifests.symptom: Generated Python code contains LLM conversation artifacts
howItManifests.impact: Syntax errors from malformed code strings

detection.name: Token Count Monitoring
detection.description: Tracks cumulative message tokens before each agent.run() call

detection.name: History Repetition Detector
detection.description: Identifies when agent repeats message content from prior turns

detection.name: Code Artifact Scanner
detection.description: Detects LLM conversation artifacts in generated Python code

fixes.name: Rolling Context Window
fixes.description: Maintains fixed-size message history by dropping oldest messages

fixes.name: Summary Compression
fixes.description: Condenses old conversation into summary messages

fixes.name: Stateful Checkpoints
fixes.description: Persists only essential state between runs, discarding verbose history

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Context Window Management for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade monitoring for Smolagents by wrapping `agent.run()` calls with `vex.verify()`. It automatically detects context overflow by tracking token counts across message history and flags repetition patterns or code contamination. When issues are detected, Vex can trigger automatic remediation through context truncation, summary generation, or checkpoint restoration.

The integration requires only `pip install smolagents vex-sdk` and a single wrapper. Vex maintains historical baselines for each agent type, alerting when context usage exceeds 80% of limits or when repetition ratios surpass thresholds. Remediation policies can auto-apply fixes or escalate to human review.

Vex dashboards provide real-time context window utilization graphs, failure pattern analysis, and one-click remediation deployment across all Smolagents instances.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex

from smolagents import CodeAgent

# Initialize Smolagents agent
agent = CodeAgent('codellama-7b')

# Vex monitors context window + auto-fixes
@vex.verify(
    failure_modes=[
        'context_overflow',
        'message_repetition',
        'code_contamination'
    ],
    auto_remediate=['truncate_context'],
    alert_threshold=0.8  # 80% context usage
)
def safe_agent_run(prompt):
    result = agent.run(prompt)
    return result

# Usage
response = safe_agent_run('Write a web scraper')
print(response)

# Vex auto-generates report:
# ✅ Context: 23k/128k tokens (18%)
# ✅ No repetition detected
# ✅ Clean code output

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK now and eliminate context window failures in your Smolagents with one wrapper: `pip install vex-sdk`
